_description: CLI-to-MCP alignment mapping. Every CLI command and every MCP tool must appear here with a classification. If a new CLI command or MCP tool is added without updating this fixture, the alignment test fails.

alignments.notes: Both create a new agent with keys
CLI 'create' signs a new document; MCP jacs_sign_document does the same
Both verify document hash, signatures, and schema
Both list agents that should sign a document
Both create an agreement for a document
Both sign the agreement section of a document
Both re-encrypt the private key with a new password
Both export an attestation as a DSSE envelope
Both rotate the agent's cryptographic keys with optional algorithm change
Both assess trust level of a remote A2A Agent Card
Both add a remote agent to the local trust store
Standalone verify command (no agent required) maps to same MCP tool
Both append an inline JACS signature block to a text/markdown file (PRD §4.1)
Both verify inline JACS signatures; both expose a strict mode (PRD §4.1, C1)
Both embed JACS signatures into PNG/JPEG/WebP metadata (PRD §4.2)
Both verify embedded image signatures with optional --key-dir / --robust LSB scan (PRD §4.2)
Both dump the JACS signed-document payload from a signed image (PRD §3.2)

cli_only.mcp_excluded_reason: Document update remains CLI-only; no generic MCP update tool exists
Setup command: creates jacs.config.json interactively, not applicable to MCP runtime
Diagnostic command: displays config to terminal, not applicable to MCP
Emits DNS TXT record commands for manual DNS provisioning; requires shell access
DNS discovery helper; could be an MCP tool but currently CLI-only
Agent self-verification; MCP agents verify implicitly on load
Discovers remote A2A agents via well-known URL; interactive CLI workflow
Starts HTTP server for A2A discovery endpoints; long-running process, not an MCP tool
Combined create-and-serve convenience; orchestration command not suitable for MCP
Extracts embedded content from JACS documents; could be an MCP tool in the future
Setup command: creates/loads persistent agent; not applicable to MCP runtime
Setup command: initializes config and agent; not applicable to MCP runtime
Meta command: starts the MCP server itself; cannot be an MCP tool
Meta command: prints version info; not applicable to MCP
Format conversion (JSON/YAML/HTML); could be an MCP tool in the future
Filesystem scan for archived keys; CLI-only diagnostic
Crash recovery; requires filesystem access, CLI-only

cli_only_feature_gated.mcp_excluded_reason: OS keychain integration; requires local system access, not applicable to MCP
OS keychain integration; requires local system access, not applicable to MCP
OS keychain integration; requires local system access, not applicable to MCP
OS keychain integration; requires local system access, not applicable to MCP

mcp_only.reason: Trust store management should be available from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs trust remove' command
Trust store management should be available from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs trust list' command
Trust store management should be available from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs trust check' command
Trust store management should be available from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs trust get' command
Search across signed documents should be available from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs search' command
Lifting signed documents to attestations could be useful from CLI; recommend adding 'jacs attest lift' command
MCP-centric: exports full agent JSON for MCP inter-agent sharing
MCP-centric: exports A2A Agent Card; CLI has 'a2a serve' for discovery instead
MCP-centric: generates well-known documents; CLI has 'a2a serve' for this
A2A artifact verification is part of MCP multi-agent workflows
A2A artifact wrapping is part of MCP multi-agent workflows
